title: Cleanup, Partial Initialization, and Error Paths · Spiral Systems Review
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the failure-atomic resource constructor contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for failure-atomic resource constructor after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.
Release acquired resources in dependency-safe reverse order with idempotent or guarded cleanup.
Use structured goto cleanup when it centralizes unwinding without skipping initialization or obscuring ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Partial Initialization State · changed case · Review Partial Initialization State
steps.instruction:
review case — A review native-systems team handles memory case 8834. Produce bounded C evidence to unwind partially acquired resources in reverse order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
